module.name: data_types

element.name: CriteriaOperators
element.short: Operators for each search criteria type

element.name: TTableType
element.short: Enumeration defining table types present in the user database
element.descr: Each table type represents a table in the database.

element.name: TableNames
element.short: Table names for each table type

element.name: TableAliases
element.short: Table aliases for each table type.

element.name: TSearchDataType
element.short: Types of data used in searches.

element.name: TCustomSearchField
element.descr: It has fields for data types, criteria, values to search, and if it is a lookup field.

element.name: TSearchField
element.short: Search field class used on advanced filters
element.descr: Inherits from TCustomSearchField and adds field name and display name.

element.name: TSortedField
element.descr: It has data for field name to sort, direction of sorting, collation, and if it is a lookup field.

element.name: TSearchGroup
element.short: Represents a group of fields used in a search
element.descr: Each group may contain multiple fields and define a conditional logic between them (AND/OR).

element.name: TCustomSearch
element.short: Structure that manage complex database searches
element.descr: It combines parameters to generate SQL queries for a database table.

element.name: SearchTypeByName
element.short: Get type for a data type name

element.name: CampoByName
element.short: Get type of field type name

element.name: LoadTablesDict
element.short: Create and load the tables dictionary (table name and table type)

element.name: LoadLocaleTablesDict
element.short: Create and load the translated tables dictionary (table type and translated table name)

element.name: TCustomSearch.RunSearch
element.short: Run the search query using the defined filters and sortings
element.descr: Returns the number of records found.

descr.p: ShowResults is a Boolean flag, if set to True, it shows a dialog with the errors or if the database integrity is OK.

element.name: TDBParams.LoadParams
element.short: Load params from connections table of the system database

element.name: TTablesDictionary
element.short: Type to map table names to table types

element.name: TLocaleTablesDictionary
element.short: Type to map table types to localized table names

element.name: TSearchField.DisplayName
element.short: Friendly field name displayed to users

element.name: TCustomSearch.GetSQLString
element.short: Generate the SQL for the query

dl.dd: Type of table to be searched.

element.name: TSearchGroup.Create
element.short: Create an instance of search group with conditional logic (AND/OR)

dl.dd: Type of logical operator used to combine fields: aoAnd or aoOr.

element.name: TSearchGroup.Destroy
element.short: Destroys the search group, freeing the allocated memory for the fields

element.name: TSearchGroup.Fields
element.short: List of fields that compose this search group

element.name: TSearchGroup.AndOr
element.short: Type of logical operator used to connect the fields of the group

module.short: Library with data management types and constants.

element.name: TValidationRules
element.short: Defines validation constraints that can be applied to imported field values.

descr.p: TValidationRules is a record that groups together all validation constraints applicable to a field during data import. These rules are used to ensure that incoming values conform to structural, semantic, and domain-specific requirements before being accepted into the target database.
The record supports several categories of validation:

ul.li: RequiredField — Indicates whether the field must contain a non-empty value.
UniqueField — Specifies that the field must contain a value not already present in the target dataset.
MaxLength — Defines the maximum allowed length for string values.
MinValue and MaxValue — Specify numeric bounds for fields that represent numbers.
MinDateTime and MaxDateTime — Define the valid range for date and time values.
FormatMask — Provides a formatting pattern that the input value must match (e.g., regular expressions or custom masks).
ValueList — Contains a comma-separated list of allowed values for enumerated fields.

descr.p: These rules are typically evaluated during the transformation and validation phase of the import pipeline, after field mapping and type conversion have been applied.

element.name: TValidationRulesHelper
element.short: Resets all validation rule fields to their default, uninitialized state.

descr.p: The Clear method initializes all fields of the TValidationRules record to their default values. This includes setting Boolean flags to False, numeric limits to zero, date limits to zero (0.0), and clearing any formatting masks or value lists.
This method is typically called when constructing or reusing a TValidationRules instance to ensure that no previous validation constraints remain active.

element.name: TRelationalField
element.short: Describes the lookup configuration for fields that reference values from another table.

descr.p: TRelationalField is a record that defines how a field in the imported dataset should resolve its value through a lookup operation in a related table. It is used to support foreign-key relationships during the import process, allowing textual or symbolic values to be converted into the corresponding primary-key identifiers of the target database.
Each field in this record specifies a different aspect of the lookup:

ul.li: LookupTable — Identifies the target table from which the lookup value should be retrieved.
LookupKeyField — Specifies the primary-key field of the lookup table.
LookupField — Defines the field in the lookup table that should be matched against the imported value.
LookupResultField — Indicates which field from the lookup table should be returned as the resolved value (typically the primary key).
DisplayFieldName — Provides a human-readable name for UI components that display lookup information.

descr.p: During the import process, these settings allow the system to translate user-provided or external identifiers (such as names, codes, or acronyms) into the internal identifiers required by the database schema. If the lookup fails, the behavior depends on the configured null-handling and error-handling rules of the associated field mapping.
